article_meta.title: Why the US needs China's rare earths16 October 2025ShareSaveAyeshea PereraAsia Digital Editor, Singapore ShareSaveGetty

reasoning: Mostly neutral summary that avoids the article’s loaded terms like “chokehold,” “betrayal,” and “economic coercion,” but it echoes one loaded phrase (“major blow”). Perspectives are underrepresented, and sources_represented is incomplete.

custom_reasoning.spin: Uses one loaded phrase (“major blow”) and mild interpretive wording (“leverage”), otherwise neutral.
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: Claims are grounded in figures and facts from the article; minor lack of attribution on CSIS points but reflect the article’s reporting.
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: “Major blow” presents an evaluative judgment as fact.
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: Slightly heightened tone via “major blow”; otherwise measured.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: No claims about intentions or mental states.
custom_reasoning.slant: Emphasizes U.S. vulnerability and China’s leverage; omits some countervailing context but not strongly one-sided.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Leaves out US officials’ quotes/accusations, environmental/processing context, and Trump-related geopolitical angles present in the article.
custom_reasoning.word_choice: Avoids politically charged terms from the source; mostly neutral vocabulary.
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Focuses on risks to the US but consistent with article emphasis.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: No discernible deference to elites vs grassroots.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: More neutral than the source overall, with one notable lapse.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Summary and sources_represented fail to include distinct perspectives (experts, US officials, CSIS, Chinese policy stance).
